articles.headline: Union Budget 2026-27 Focuses on Green Growth, Digital Public Infrastructure, and Fiscal Consolidation
articles.explainer:
• Finance Minister presented the Union Budget 2026-27, emphasizing a balanced approach towards economic growth, environmental sustainability, and fiscal prudence.
• Key allocations include significant boosts for green hydrogen mission, renewable energy projects, and climate-resilient infrastructure.
• Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) received enhanced funding, aiming to expand reach of digital services in health, education, and financial inclusion.
• Government projected a fiscal deficit of 4.2% for FY27, signaling commitment to the FRBM roadmap, alongside measures to boost tax compliance and non-tax revenues.
• New schemes announced for rural employment generation and skill development, targeting vulnerable sections of society.

articles.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Concurrent List Legislation, Reinforces Federal Structure
articles.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment clarifying the scope of legislative powers between the Centre and States on subjects listed in the Concurrent List.
• The ruling emphasized the principle of cooperative federalism, stating that Central laws should not unduly encroach upon the legislative domain of states, especially when state laws exist.
• The case originated from a challenge by several states against a recent Central act on agricultural market reforms, citing violation of their constitutional autonomy.
• The Court laid down a 'doctrine of harmonious interpretation' to resolve conflicts, prioritizing state legislation where it serves specific local needs without contradicting the fundamental purpose of the Central law.
• Experts suggest this judgment could reshape the legislative process, requiring greater consultation between the Centre and States.

Discuss how the 'doctrine of harmonious interpretation' can be applied to resolve conflicts in legislative competence.
Evaluate the historical context and evolution of federal disputes in India concerning the Concurrent List.
Examine the challenges in implementing Central laws on subjects where states have strong legislative interests.

articles.trivia: The Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution lists three lists: Union List, State List, and Concurrent List.

articles.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Maritime Security and Digital Economy
articles.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue in New Delhi concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing enhanced cooperation in maritime security, digital economy, and resilient supply chains.
• Both sides committed to upholding international law, particularly UNCLOS, in the Indo-Pacific region, addressing concerns over regional stability.
• New initiatives were launched to promote digital trade, foster innovation, and build capacity in cybersecurity among ASEAN member states.
• Discussions also covered improving connectivity through land and sea routes, including progress on the India-Myanmar-Thailand Trilateral Highway.
• The dialogue reaffirmed the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and India's 'Act East' policy.

articles.headline: National Quantum Mission Achieves Key Milestones in Quantum Computing and Communication
articles.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology announced significant progress under the National Quantum Mission (NQM), with several prototypes developed in quantum computing and communication.
• Indian researchers successfully demonstrated a 64-qubit quantum processor prototype, marking a major leap in indigenous quantum technology development.
• A secure quantum communication link over a 150 km distance was established, paving the way for advanced cybersecurity applications.
• NQM aims to develop quantum technologies and associated applications, fostering a robust ecosystem for quantum science and engineering.
• The mission involves collaboration between academia, industry, and government research institutions, positioning India as a global leader in quantum tech.

articles.trivia: Quantum mechanics, the foundation of quantum computing, was developed in the early 20th century to explain the behavior of matter and energy at the atomic and subatomic levels.

articles.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Initiative Launched to Tackle Growing Monsoon Challenges
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched a comprehensive 'National Urban Flood Resilience Initiative' across 50 major cities prone to recurrent flooding.
• The initiative integrates advanced early warning systems, real-time data analytics, and improved urban drainage infrastructure to mitigate flood impact.
• Focus includes nature-based solutions like permeable pavements, urban wetlands, and restoration of natural water bodies to enhance water absorption capacity.
• It mandates revision of urban planning norms to incorporate flood-resilient design principles and strict enforcement of building codes in flood-prone areas.
• Community engagement and capacity building for disaster response teams are also key components, aiming for a multi-pronged approach.

articles.trivia: Chennai, Mumbai, and Kolkata are among the Indian cities most vulnerable to urban flooding due to their coastal location and rapid unplanned urbanization.

articles.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Foundational Learning, Challenges in Higher Education Reforms
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive report reviewing the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 after six years.
• The report noted significant progress in foundational literacy and numeracy programs, with improved enrollment rates in early childhood care and education.
• Key challenges remain in the higher education sector, particularly regarding the multidisciplinary approach, flexible curriculum, and faculty recruitment.
• Digital education initiatives, especially through DIKSHA and SWAYAM platforms, have seen widespread adoption, bridging learning gaps.
• The review recommends accelerating reforms in teacher training, curriculum development for vocational education, and greater autonomy for higher education institutions.

articles.headline: India's First Private Sector Space Station Module Successfully Launched
articles.explainer:
• A major milestone for India's private space sector as the first indigenously developed private space station module, 'Vyom Mitra-1', was successfully launched.
• Developed by a consortium of Indian startups, the module aims to provide a platform for microgravity research and technology demonstrations.
• This launch signifies the growing capabilities of India's private space industry and aligns with the government's push for private participation in space.
• The module will orbit Earth at a low-Earth orbit (LEO) and is expected to host experiments from various scientific institutions and private companies.
• ISRO provided technical support and launch vehicle assistance, demonstrating a successful public-private partnership model.

articles.headline: New Foreign Trade Policy 2026-31 Unveiled, Aims for Export Boost and Ease of Doing Business
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched the new Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2026-31, focusing on making India a global export hub.
• Key features include simplified procedures for exporters, integration of technology for paperless transactions, and enhanced support for MSMEs.
• The policy introduces new schemes to promote exports of green products and services, aligning with India's climate goals.
• Efforts to diversify India's export basket and explore new markets in Africa and Latin America were highlighted.
• The FTP aims to achieve a merchandise and services export target of $2 trillion by 2030, leveraging existing trade agreements and forging new ones.

articles.headline: India's First Seaweed Park Inaugurated in Tamil Nadu to Boost Marine Biotechnology
articles.explainer:
• The nation's first dedicated 'Seaweed Park' was inaugurated in Ramanathapuram district, Tamil Nadu, aiming to promote seaweed cultivation and processing.
• The park will serve as a hub for research and development, skill training, and value addition in the burgeoning marine biotechnology sector.
• Seaweed cultivation offers significant economic potential for coastal communities, providing an alternative livelihood and boosting the 'Blue Economy'.
• It also has environmental benefits, including carbon sequestration and improving marine ecosystem health.
• The initiative is part of a larger government push to harness marine resources sustainably and develop a robust aquaculture sector.

articles.headline: India Deploys Advanced Indigenous Anti-Drone Systems Along Western Borders
articles.explainer:
• India has deployed a new generation of indigenously developed anti-drone systems along its western borders, enhancing capabilities against unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) threats.
• The systems incorporate advanced radar, electronic warfare countermeasures, and kinetic kill options to detect, track, and neutralize hostile drones.
• This deployment is a response to increasing instances of drone intrusions for smuggling arms, drugs, and reconnaissance activities.
• Developed by DRDO in collaboration with private sector partners, it signifies a major step towards self-reliance in defense technology.
• Training programs for border security forces have been intensified to effectively operate and maintain these sophisticated systems.

articles.headline: Digital Health ID Adoption Crosses 500 Million Mark, Revolutionizing Healthcare Access
articles.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced that over 500 million Digital Health IDs (ABHA - Ayushman Bharat Health Account) have been created across India.
• This milestone signifies a major step towards digitizing India's healthcare ecosystem, enabling seamless access to medical records and services.
• The ABHA ID allows individuals to link their health records, facilitating easier consultations, prescription management, and insurance claims.
• The mission aims to create an integrated digital health infrastructure, improving efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of healthcare services.
• Challenges remain in ensuring data privacy, interoperability across all healthcare providers, and digital literacy among diverse populations.

articles.headline: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meeting Preparations Underway in Bengaluru
articles.explainer:
• Preparations are in full swing for the upcoming G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting scheduled for later this month in Bengaluru.
• India, as the current G20 President, is hosting the meeting which will focus on global economic stability, sustainable finance, and international taxation.
• Key agendas include addressing global debt vulnerabilities, strengthening multilateral development banks, and fostering climate finance mechanisms.
• Discussions are also expected on regulatory frameworks for cryptocurrencies and enhancing financial inclusion through digital public infrastructure.
• The meeting aims to build consensus on critical global economic challenges and coordinate policy responses among member nations.

articles.headline: India Adds Record Solar Energy Capacity in FY26, Surpassing National Targets
articles.explainer:
• India achieved a record addition of solar energy capacity in Fiscal Year 2025-26, significantly surpassing its national targets for renewable energy deployment.
• This surge is attributed to robust government policies, increased private investment, and declining costs of solar technology.
• The growth is crucial for India to meet its ambitious climate targets and achieve energy security, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, energy storage solutions, and land acquisition for large-scale solar projects.
• The government plans further incentives for domestic manufacturing of solar components and innovative financing models for rooftop solar.

articles.headline: Government Explores Revival of Historic Trade Routes for Regional Connectivity and Tourism
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs, in collaboration with the Ministry of Tourism and Culture, is exploring the revival of ancient and historic trade routes.
• The initiative aims to boost regional connectivity, foster economic ties, and promote cultural tourism along routes like parts of the Silk Road and Spice Route.
• Feasibility studies are underway for infrastructure development, including road and rail links, and cross-border customs facilitation.
• The project emphasizes cultural exchange and heritage preservation, involving local communities and promoting sustainable tourism practices.
• This move is seen as part of India's broader strategy to enhance its geopolitical and geoeconomic influence in its extended neighborhood.

articles.headline: Government to Introduce New Policy Framework for Social Security of Gig Workers
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Labour and Employment announced plans to introduce a new policy framework aimed at providing social security benefits to gig and platform workers.
• The proposed policy will address issues such as health insurance, provident fund, and accident coverage for these workers, who currently lack traditional employment benefits.
• Consultations are ongoing with various stakeholders, including platform companies, worker unions, and legal experts, to draft a comprehensive and equitable framework.
• The move acknowledges the growing gig economy and the need to protect the rights and welfare of a significant portion of the workforce.
• Challenges include defining 'gig worker' precisely, ensuring compliance from diverse platforms, and developing sustainable funding models for benefits.

articles.headline: India Plans Expansion of Arctic Research Station 'Himadri' Amidst Growing Geopolitical Interest
articles.explainer:
• India announced plans to expand its permanent Arctic research station, 'Himadri', reflecting growing scientific and geopolitical interest in the polar region.
• The expansion includes upgraded infrastructure for year-round operations, enhanced research facilities, and increased capacity for scientists.
• Research focuses on climate change impacts, glaciology, oceanography, and atmospheric sciences relevant to the Indian monsoon.
• This move comes as the Arctic region gains strategic importance due to melting ice caps opening new shipping routes and access to natural resources.
• India is an observer state in the Arctic Council and emphasizes peaceful scientific cooperation in the region.

articles.headline: Gender Budgeting Impact Assessment Reveals Mixed Results, Calls for Enhanced Implementation
articles.explainer:
• A government-commissioned impact assessment of Gender Budgeting across various ministries and states revealed mixed results in achieving gender equality outcomes.
• While awareness of gender budgeting has increased, the report highlighted gaps in actual expenditure tracking and the integration of a gender perspective in policy formulation.
• Positive impacts were noted in schemes directly targeting women's health and education, but less so in infrastructure and economic empowerment programs.
• The assessment recommends strengthening capacity building for officials, improving data collection mechanisms, and mandating gender impact assessments for all new policies.
• Gender budgeting aims to analyze government budgets through a gender lens to ensure equitable allocation of resources and address gender disparities.

articles.headline: New Policy Framework Introduced to Boost Circular Economy and Waste-to-Wealth Initiatives
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change unveiled a new national policy framework aimed at accelerating India's transition to a circular economy.
• The policy focuses on reducing waste generation, promoting reuse, repair, and recycling across various sectors, particularly electronics, plastics, and construction.
• It introduces incentives for industries adopting circular economy principles and mandates Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for more product categories.
• Emphasis is placed on 'waste-to-wealth' initiatives, encouraging innovation in resource recovery and sustainable material management.
• The framework aims to mitigate environmental pollution, conserve natural resources, and create green jobs, aligning with India's sustainable development goals.

articles.headline: Pilot Project on AI-Driven Precision Agriculture Launched in Drought-Prone Regions
articles.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched a pilot project on Artificial Intelligence (AI)-driven precision agriculture in select drought-prone regions.
• The initiative utilizes AI and machine learning models to provide farmers with real-time data on soil health, weather patterns, and crop disease prediction.
• Smart sensors and satellite imagery are integrated to optimize irrigation, fertilizer use, and pest management, aiming to enhance crop yield and reduce input costs.
• The project also explores AI-powered solutions for market price prediction and supply chain optimization, benefiting farmers' incomes.
• Challenges include digital literacy among farmers, data infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access to technology.

prelimsMocks.question:
With reference to the Union Budget 2026-27, consider the following statements:
1. The budget projects a fiscal deficit of 4.2% for the financial year 2026-27.
2. It includes significant allocations for the National Green Hydrogen Mission.
3. Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) receives reduced funding compared to previous years.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct as the budget projects a fiscal deficit of 4.2% for FY27. Statement 2 is correct as significant boosts for green hydrogen mission and renewable energy projects were announced. Statement 3 is incorrect as Digital Public Infrastructure (DPI) received enhanced funding, not reduced.

prelimsMocks.question:
The Supreme Court's recent ruling on legislative powers in the Concurrent List emphasized which of the following principles?
(a) Doctrine of Absolute Central Supremacy
(b) Doctrine of Harmonious Interpretation
(c) Doctrine of Implied Powers
(d) Doctrine of Prospective Overruling
prelimsMocks.explanation: The Supreme Court laid down a 'doctrine of harmonious interpretation' to resolve conflicts between Central and State laws on Concurrent List subjects, prioritizing state legislation where it serves specific local needs without contradicting the fundamental purpose of the Central law.

prelimsMocks.question: Which of the following is the name of India's permanent Arctic research station?
prelimsMocks.explanation: India's permanent Arctic research station is named 'Himadri'. Bharati and Maitri are India's research stations in Antarctica, while Dakshin Gangotri was India's first research base in Antarctica, now buried under ice.

prelimsMocks.question:
Consider the following statements regarding the National Quantum Mission (NQM):
1. NQM aims to develop quantum technologies and associated applications.
2. A 64-qubit quantum processor prototype has been successfully demonstrated under NQM.
3. Quantum communication links established under NQM are primarily for civilian entertainment purposes.
Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
prelimsMocks.explanation: Statement 1 is correct, as NQM aims to develop quantum technologies. Statement 2 is correct, as a 64-qubit quantum processor prototype was demonstrated. Statement 3 is incorrect; quantum communication links under NQM are established for secure communication and cybersecurity applications, not primarily for entertainment.

prelimsMocks.question: What is the primary objective of the 'National Urban Flood Resilience Initiative' recently launched by the Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs?
options: (a) To promote inter-state river linking projects to divert floodwaters.
(b) To integrate advanced early warning systems and improve urban drainage infrastructure for flood mitigation.
(c) To relocate urban populations from flood-prone areas to higher ground.
(d) To ban all construction activities in coastal cities to prevent future flooding.
To integrate advanced early warning systems and improve urban drainage infrastructure for flood mitigation.
prelimsMocks.answer: (b) To integrate advanced early warning systems and improve urban drainage infrastructure for flood mitigation.
prelimsMocks.explanation: The primary objective of the National Urban Flood Resilience Initiative is to integrate advanced early warning systems, real-time data analytics, and improved urban drainage infrastructure to mitigate flood impact in major cities.

mainsMocks.question: The Union Budget 2026-27 outlines a vision for 'Green Growth' and enhanced 'Digital Public Infrastructure'. Analyze how these two pillars are crucial for India's sustainable and inclusive economic development. What challenges might impede their effective implementation?

GS1.headline: Government Explores Revival of Historic Trade Routes for Regional Connectivity and Tourism
GS1.explainer:
• The Ministry of External Affairs, in collaboration with the Ministry of Tourism and Culture, is exploring the revival of ancient and historic trade routes.
• The initiative aims to boost regional connectivity, foster economic ties, and promote cultural tourism along routes like parts of the Silk Road and Spice Route.
• Feasibility studies are underway for infrastructure development, including road and rail links, and cross-border customs facilitation.
• The project emphasizes cultural exchange and heritage preservation, involving local communities and promoting sustainable tourism practices.
• This move is seen as part of India's broader strategy to enhance its geopolitical and geoeconomic influence in its extended neighborhood.

GS2.headline: Supreme Court Upholds States' Rights in Concurrent List Legislation, Reinforces Federal Structure
GS2.explainer:
• The Supreme Court delivered a landmark judgment clarifying the scope of legislative powers between the Centre and States on subjects listed in the Concurrent List.
• The ruling emphasized the principle of cooperative federalism, stating that Central laws should not unduly encroach upon the legislative domain of states, especially when state laws exist.
• The case originated from a challenge by several states against a recent Central act on agricultural market reforms, citing violation of their constitutional autonomy.
• The Court laid down a 'doctrine of harmonious interpretation' to resolve conflicts, prioritizing state legislation where it serves specific local needs without contradicting the fundamental purpose of the Central law.
• Experts suggest this judgment could reshape the legislative process, requiring greater consultation between the Centre and States.

Evaluate the historical context and evolution of federal disputes in India concerning the Concurrent List.

GS2.trivia: The Seventh Schedule of the Indian Constitution lists three lists: Union List, State List, and Concurrent List.

GS2.headline: India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue Concludes with Focus on Maritime Security and Digital Economy
GS2.explainer:
• The annual India-ASEAN Strategic Dialogue in New Delhi concluded with a joint declaration emphasizing enhanced cooperation in maritime security, digital economy, and resilient supply chains.
• Both sides committed to upholding international law, particularly UNCLOS, in the Indo-Pacific region, addressing concerns over regional stability.
• New initiatives were launched to promote digital trade, foster innovation, and build capacity in cybersecurity among ASEAN member states.
• Discussions also covered improving connectivity through land and sea routes, including progress on the India-Myanmar-Thailand Trilateral Highway.
• The dialogue reaffirmed the importance of a free, open, and inclusive Indo-Pacific and India's 'Act East' policy.

GS2.headline: NEP 2020 Implementation Review Highlights Progress in Foundational Learning, Challenges in Higher Education Reforms
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Education released a comprehensive report reviewing the implementation of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 after six years.
• The report noted significant progress in foundational literacy and numeracy programs, with improved enrollment rates in early childhood care and education.
• Key challenges remain in the higher education sector, particularly regarding the multidisciplinary approach, flexible curriculum, and faculty recruitment.
• Digital education initiatives, especially through DIKSHA and SWAYAM platforms, have seen widespread adoption, bridging learning gaps.
• The review recommends accelerating reforms in teacher training, curriculum development for vocational education, and greater autonomy for higher education institutions.

GS2.headline: Digital Health ID Adoption Crosses 500 Million Mark, Revolutionizing Healthcare Access
GS2.explainer:
• The Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM) announced that over 500 million Digital Health IDs (ABHA - Ayushman Bharat Health Account) have been created across India.
• This milestone signifies a major step towards digitizing India's healthcare ecosystem, enabling seamless access to medical records and services.
• The ABHA ID allows individuals to link their health records, facilitating easier consultations, prescription management, and insurance claims.
• The mission aims to create an integrated digital health infrastructure, improving efficiency, transparency, and accessibility of healthcare services.
• Challenges remain in ensuring data privacy, interoperability across all healthcare providers, and digital literacy among diverse populations.

GS2.headline: G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors Meeting Preparations Underway in Bengaluru
GS2.explainer:
• Preparations are in full swing for the upcoming G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors (FMCBG) meeting scheduled for later this month in Bengaluru.
• India, as the current G20 President, is hosting the meeting which will focus on global economic stability, sustainable finance, and international taxation.
• Key agendas include addressing global debt vulnerabilities, strengthening multilateral development banks, and fostering climate finance mechanisms.
• Discussions are also expected on regulatory frameworks for cryptocurrencies and enhancing financial inclusion through digital public infrastructure.
• The meeting aims to build consensus on critical global economic challenges and coordinate policy responses among member nations.

GS2.headline: Government to Introduce New Policy Framework for Social Security of Gig Workers
GS2.explainer:
• The Ministry of Labour and Employment announced plans to introduce a new policy framework aimed at providing social security benefits to gig and platform workers.
• The proposed policy will address issues such as health insurance, provident fund, and accident coverage for these workers, who currently lack traditional employment benefits.
• Consultations are ongoing with various stakeholders, including platform companies, worker unions, and legal experts, to draft a comprehensive and equitable framework.
• The move acknowledges the growing gig economy and the need to protect the rights and welfare of a significant portion of the workforce.
• Challenges include defining 'gig worker' precisely, ensuring compliance from diverse platforms, and developing sustainable funding models for benefits.

GS2.headline: Gender Budgeting Impact Assessment Reveals Mixed Results, Calls for Enhanced Implementation
GS2.explainer:
• A government-commissioned impact assessment of Gender Budgeting across various ministries and states revealed mixed results in achieving gender equality outcomes.
• While awareness of gender budgeting has increased, the report highlighted gaps in actual expenditure tracking and the integration of a gender perspective in policy formulation.
• Positive impacts were noted in schemes directly targeting women's health and education, but less so in infrastructure and economic empowerment programs.
• The assessment recommends strengthening capacity building for officials, improving data collection mechanisms, and mandating gender impact assessments for all new policies.
• Gender budgeting aims to analyze government budgets through a gender lens to ensure equitable allocation of resources and address gender disparities.

• Key allocations include significant boosts for green hydrogen mission, renewable energy projects, and climate-resilient infrastructure.

GS3.headline: National Quantum Mission Achieves Key Milestones in Quantum Computing and Communication
GS3.explainer:
• The Department of Science & Technology announced significant progress under the National Quantum Mission (NQM), with several prototypes developed in quantum computing and communication.
• Indian researchers successfully demonstrated a 64-qubit quantum processor prototype, marking a major leap in indigenous quantum technology development.
• A secure quantum communication link over a 150 km distance was established, paving the way for advanced cybersecurity applications.
• NQM aims to develop quantum technologies and associated applications, fostering a robust ecosystem for quantum science and engineering.
• The mission involves collaboration between academia, industry, and government research institutions, positioning India as a global leader in quantum tech.

GS3.headline: National Urban Flood Resilience Initiative Launched to Tackle Growing Monsoon Challenges
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Housing and Urban Affairs launched a comprehensive 'National Urban Flood Resilience Initiative' across 50 major cities prone to recurrent flooding.
• The initiative integrates advanced early warning systems, real-time data analytics, and improved urban drainage infrastructure to mitigate flood impact.
• Focus includes nature-based solutions like permeable pavements, urban wetlands, and restoration of natural water bodies to enhance water absorption capacity.
• It mandates revision of urban planning norms to incorporate flood-resilient design principles and strict enforcement of building codes in flood-prone areas.
• Community engagement and capacity building for disaster response teams are also key components, aiming for a multi-pronged approach.

GS3.headline: India's First Private Sector Space Station Module Successfully Launched
GS3.explainer:
• A major milestone for India's private space sector as the first indigenously developed private space station module, 'Vyom Mitra-1', was successfully launched.
• Developed by a consortium of Indian startups, the module aims to provide a platform for microgravity research and technology demonstrations.
• This launch signifies the growing capabilities of India's private space industry and aligns with the government's push for private participation in space.
• The module will orbit Earth at a low-Earth orbit (LEO) and is expected to host experiments from various scientific institutions and private companies.
• ISRO provided technical support and launch vehicle assistance, demonstrating a successful public-private partnership model.

GS3.headline: New Foreign Trade Policy 2026-31 Unveiled, Aims for Export Boost and Ease of Doing Business
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry launched the new Foreign Trade Policy (FTP) 2026-31, focusing on making India a global export hub.
• Key features include simplified procedures for exporters, integration of technology for paperless transactions, and enhanced support for MSMEs.
• The policy introduces new schemes to promote exports of green products and services, aligning with India's climate goals.
• Efforts to diversify India's export basket and explore new markets in Africa and Latin America were highlighted.
• The FTP aims to achieve a merchandise and services export target of $2 trillion by 2030, leveraging existing trade agreements and forging new ones.

GS3.headline: India's First Seaweed Park Inaugurated in Tamil Nadu to Boost Marine Biotechnology
GS3.explainer:
• The nation's first dedicated 'Seaweed Park' was inaugurated in Ramanathapuram district, Tamil Nadu, aiming to promote seaweed cultivation and processing.
• The park will serve as a hub for research and development, skill training, and value addition in the burgeoning marine biotechnology sector.
• Seaweed cultivation offers significant economic potential for coastal communities, providing an alternative livelihood and boosting the 'Blue Economy'.
• It also has environmental benefits, including carbon sequestration and improving marine ecosystem health.
• The initiative is part of a larger government push to harness marine resources sustainably and develop a robust aquaculture sector.

GS3.headline: India Deploys Advanced Indigenous Anti-Drone Systems Along Western Borders
GS3.explainer:
• India has deployed a new generation of indigenously developed anti-drone systems along its western borders, enhancing capabilities against unmanned aerial vehicle (UAV) threats.
• The systems incorporate advanced radar, electronic warfare countermeasures, and kinetic kill options to detect, track, and neutralize hostile drones.
• This deployment is a response to increasing instances of drone intrusions for smuggling arms, drugs, and reconnaissance activities.
• Developed by DRDO in collaboration with private sector partners, it signifies a major step towards self-reliance in defense technology.
• Training programs for border security forces have been intensified to effectively operate and maintain these sophisticated systems.

GS3.headline: India Adds Record Solar Energy Capacity in FY26, Surpassing National Targets
GS3.explainer:
• India achieved a record addition of solar energy capacity in Fiscal Year 2025-26, significantly surpassing its national targets for renewable energy deployment.
• This surge is attributed to robust government policies, increased private investment, and declining costs of solar technology.
• The growth is crucial for India to meet its ambitious climate targets and achieve energy security, reducing reliance on fossil fuels.
• Challenges remain in grid integration, energy storage solutions, and land acquisition for large-scale solar projects.
• The government plans further incentives for domestic manufacturing of solar components and innovative financing models for rooftop solar.

GS3.headline: India Plans Expansion of Arctic Research Station 'Himadri' Amidst Growing Geopolitical Interest
GS3.explainer:
• India announced plans to expand its permanent Arctic research station, 'Himadri', reflecting growing scientific and geopolitical interest in the polar region.
• The expansion includes upgraded infrastructure for year-round operations, enhanced research facilities, and increased capacity for scientists.
• Research focuses on climate change impacts, glaciology, oceanography, and atmospheric sciences relevant to the Indian monsoon.
• This move comes as the Arctic region gains strategic importance due to melting ice caps opening new shipping routes and access to natural resources.
• India is an observer state in the Arctic Council and emphasizes peaceful scientific cooperation in the region.

GS3.headline: New Policy Framework Introduced to Boost Circular Economy and Waste-to-Wealth Initiatives
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Environment, Forest and Climate Change unveiled a new national policy framework aimed at accelerating India's transition to a circular economy.
• The policy focuses on reducing waste generation, promoting reuse, repair, and recycling across various sectors, particularly electronics, plastics, and construction.
• It introduces incentives for industries adopting circular economy principles and mandates Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) for more product categories.
• Emphasis is placed on 'waste-to-wealth' initiatives, encouraging innovation in resource recovery and sustainable material management.
• The framework aims to mitigate environmental pollution, conserve natural resources, and create green jobs, aligning with India's sustainable development goals.

GS3.headline: Pilot Project on AI-Driven Precision Agriculture Launched in Drought-Prone Regions
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers' Welfare launched a pilot project on Artificial Intelligence (AI)-driven precision agriculture in select drought-prone regions.
• The initiative utilizes AI and machine learning models to provide farmers with real-time data on soil health, weather patterns, and crop disease prediction.
• Smart sensors and satellite imagery are integrated to optimize irrigation, fertilizer use, and pest management, aiming to enhance crop yield and reduce input costs.
• The project also explores AI-powered solutions for market price prediction and supply chain optimization, benefiting farmers' incomes.
• Challenges include digital literacy among farmers, data infrastructure development, and ensuring equitable access to technology.
